prompt: Evaluate kolm's pricing/packaging framing for an AI-agent vendor buyer in 2026. Tiers: free Scan (watermarked preview), $750 one-time Signed Readiness Report, Continuous $299/mo (re-attested weekly) and $999/mo (re-attested every deploy + injection regression), $15k Full Readiness (guided engagement), $25k Reviewed Attestation (named human co-signer). Is the free-scan -> one-time -> continuous -> high-touch ladder the right shape? Is differentiating the two Continuous tiers on CADENCE (weekly vs every-deploy) + DEPTH the right move (we just removed an 'up to 3 vs 15 agents' meter)? Compare to how 2026 security/devtools companies package self-serve + sales-assisted (cite real pricing pages/URLs). Recommend any changes to the FRAMING/labels/order (NOT the locked prices) that would convert better, and the one-line value statement to put atop the pricing page.
content:
**The proposed ladder (free scan → one-time signed report → continuous subscriptions → high-touch) is the right shape for an AI-agent security/readiness vendor targeting 2026 buyers.** It mirrors proven patterns in security/devtools SaaS: low-friction entry for evaluation/PLG, clear upgrade paths that expand usage/commitment, and a transition to sales-assisted for complex/enterprise needs.[[1]](https://snyk.io/plans/)

### Comparison to 2026 Security/Devtools Packaging
Real-world examples (as of mid-2026) show consistent structures:

- **Snyk** (code/open-source/container security): Free tier (limited) → Team (~$25–$98 per contributing developer/month, self-serve) → Enterprise (custom quote, demo/booked). Strong self-serve ramp with usage-based elements; high-touch for scale/compliance.[[1]](https://snyk.io/plans/)

- **SonarQube/SonarSource**: Free (limited LOC) → Team (starts ~$32/month, LOC-based) → Enterprise (custom pricing, advanced features/audit). Self-managed or cloud options with clear self-serve entry.[[2]](https://www.sonarsource.com/plans-and-pricing/)

- **GitHub Advanced Security**: Free on public repos → Paid per-active-committer/month tiers (Secret Protection ~$19, Code Security ~$30) for private/enterprise. Metered/self-serve scaling.[[3]](https://github.com/security/plans)

- **Lakera** (AI/GenAI security): Free Community (limited requests) → Enterprise (custom/sales contact for advanced features).[[4]](https://platform.lakera.ai/pricing)

- **Mindgard** (AI red teaming/security testing): Free sandbox/labs → Premium/Enterprise (sales contact).[[5]](https://sourceforge.net/software/product/Mindgard/)

These emphasize **self-serve starters** (free or low-commitment to hook developers/teams) transitioning to **per-user/usage subscriptions**, with **enterprise/high-touch** (custom pricing, demos, named support, compliance features) gated behind sales. Kolm’s mix of one-time + recurring + high-touch engagements fits well, especially for AI-specific attestation/readiness where buyers range from indie devs/agents to regulated enterprises needing human sign-off. The cadence/depth differentiation (post-removal of the 3-vs-15 agents meter) is a **strong move**—it ties directly to buyer pain (how fresh/reliable is the signal?) rather than arbitrary caps, which often feel punitive in security tools.

### Recommended Framing/Label/Order Changes (Prices Locked)
The core ladder is sound and converts well by reducing friction early while creating natural expansion. Minor tweaks improve clarity, perceived value progression, and psychological flow (self-serve first, then assisted):

1. **Reorder/group slightly for better conversion**:
   - Self-serve section first (Free Scan + One-time + the two Continuous tiers).
   - Then “Enterprise & Attestation Services” for the $15k/$25k (positions high-touch as premium add-ons, not the main path).

2. **Refine labels** (make benefits/outcomes clearer and more benefit-oriented; avoid overly technical jargon like “injection regression” if the audience is broad AI vendors):
   - Free: “Free Preview Scan” (or keep “Scan” but add “(watermarked)”).
   - One-time: “Signed Readiness Report – $750 one-time” (strong as-is; signals quick, professional output).
   - Continuous tiers:
     - Lower ($299/mo): “Continuous Monitoring – Weekly Re-attestation”.
     - Higher ($999/mo): “Production Continuous – Deploy-time + Regression Re-attestation”.
     - (Or “Starter Continuous” / “Pro Continuous” if you want simpler branding; cadence + depth is already the right axis.)
   - $15k: “Full Readiness Engagement – Guided Implementation”.
   - $25k: “Reviewed Attestation – Named Human Co-signer”.

3. **Other framing notes**:
   - Add subtle “Most Popular” or “Recommended for Production” badge on the $999/mo tier to guide choice.
   - Use comparison table (or clear columns) highlighting cadence, depth, and what each unlocks (e.g., “Fresh weekly signal” vs. “Every-deploy gate + regression coverage”).
   - Ensure the page supports self-serve checkout for the lower tiers (credit card) while routing $15k/$25k to a form/demo.

These changes keep the progression intuitive, emphasize outcomes (preview → validated report → always-on assurance → enterprise-grade attestation), and align with how buyers evaluate (start free, prove value one-time, then subscribe for ongoing).

**One-line value statement for the top of the pricing page**:  
“Instantly preview, validate, and continuously attest the security and readiness of your AI agents—from free scans to production-grade, human-co-signed reports.” 

This is concise, benefit-focused, covers the full ladder, and speaks directly to AI-agent vendor buyers in 2026.
